localizedDescriptions.en: Set of medication orders communicated to pharmacy.
An identifier for the prescription as a whole.
Instructions for use of a medication, vaccine or other therapeutic item.
Order.
Identification of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic item being ordered.

annotations.comment: It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication item' be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. The extent of coding may vary from the simple name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack to be used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available.

localizedDescriptions.en: The strength and form of the medication substance, including details of specific ingredients where required by an ad-hoc preparation or infusion.
The name of the medication substance. This item should be coded if possible.

annotations.comment: Usage of substance name will vary according to context of use.

children.name: Pharmacecuetical dose form

localizedDescriptions.en: The formulation or presentation of the medication.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication_substance.v0 and name/value='Preparation']/items[at0071 and name/value='Pharmacecuetical dose form']/value

children.name: Strength of medicinal product

localizedDescriptions.en: The value of the strength of medication as a real number.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication_substance.v0 and name/value='Preparation']/items[at0115 and name/value='Strength of medicinal product']/value

children.name: Strength of medicinal product unit

localizedDescriptions.en: The dose unit of the medication substance strength.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.medication_substance.v0 and name/value='Preparation']/items[at0116 and name/value='Strength of medicinal product unit']/value

annotations.comment: e.g 'tablet','mg'. Often coded using a reference terminology such as SNOMED CT.
This is commonly carried as part of the medicine name and provided by the medicines terminology but in some cases must be specified separately.

localizedDescriptions.en: The route of administration.

annotations.comment:
For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'. 
Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Multiple potential routes may be specified.

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about a dose direction for the medication order.

children.name: Number of units per intake

localizedDescriptions.en: The combination of a medication amount associated with a single medication timing.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']

children.name: Pattern sequence

localizedDescriptions.en: The intended sequence of this dose pattern within the overall sequence of dose directions.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0164]/value

children.name: Dose

localizedDescriptions.en: The value of the amount of medication as a real number.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0144 and name/value='Dose']

children.name: Dose

localizedDescriptions.en: The value of the amount of medication as a real number.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0144 and name/value='Dose']/value

children.name: Dose

localizedDescriptions.en: The value of the amount of medication as a real number.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0144 and name/value='Dose']/value

children.name: upper
children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0144 and name/value='Dose']/value/upper

children.name: lower
children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0144 and name/value='Dose']/value/lower

children.name: Dose unit

localizedDescriptions.en: The dose unit of the amount of medication.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0056]/items[at0058 and name/value='Number of units per intake']/items[at0145]/value

annotations.comment: For example: 'tablet','mg'. Coding of the dose unit with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

localizedDescriptions.en: Structured information about the timing (intended or actual) of administration or use of a medicine, other therapeutic good or other intervention that is given on a scheduled basis.
The frequency as number of times per time period (limited to a single day) that the activity is to take place.
The frequency as number of times per time period (limited to a single day) that the activity is to take place.

annotations.comment: e.g. "4 times per day" or ""3 to 4 times per hour"

localizedDescriptions.en: The frequency as number of times per time period (limited to a single day) that the activity is to take place.

annotations.comment: e.g. "4 times per day" or ""3 to 4 times per hour"
e.g. "4 times per day" or ""3 to 4 times per hour"

localizedDescriptions.en: The time interval or minimum and maximum range of an interval between each scheduled activity, limited to a single day.
A specific time or interval of time  during a single day when the activity should occur.
A specific time or interval of time  during a single day when the activity should occur.
A specific time or interval of time  during a single day when the activity should occur.
A specific, named time event within a single day, when the activity should occur.
Perform the activity immediately.
Perform the activity in the morning.
Perform the activity at night.

list.label: in the morning and at night

localizedLabels.en: in the morning and at night

localizedDescriptions.en: Perform the activity in the morning and at night.

annotations.comment:
e.g. before each meal, at bedtime, in the morning.
It is understood that these terms may not equate to the same exact times in different cultures.

localizedDescriptions.en: The activity should only occur when the "as required" trigger condition is met.
The condition which triggers an 'as required' activity.

annotations.comment: For example: '2 tablets at 6pm' or '20mg three times per day'. Please note: this cluster allows multiple occurrences to enable representation of a complete set of dose patterns for a single dose direction.

localizedDescriptions.en: The duration of this dose direction.
The duration of this dose direction.
The direction should be continued indefinitely.
The direction should be continued indefinitely and discontinuation is not recommended.
The duration of this dose direction.

annotations.comment: For example: '1 tab in the morning, 1 tab at night, for 3 weeks, on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays'. This cluster allows multiple occurrences to enable representation of a complete set of dose directions for a single medication order.

localizedDescriptions.en: An additional instruction directed primarily at the patient or carers.

annotations.comment: For example: 'To reduce your blood pressure', 'To thin your blood'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible. If required it is possible to use a LINK attribute to associate this element with, for example, the 'original diagnosis' in a separate Problem list composition but the indication should be explicitly recorded here, as the link target may change over time.

localizedDescriptions.en: Details about the intended course of the medication.
The date and optional time to commence use of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic good.

children.name: Date of end of treatment

localizedDescriptions.en: The date and optional time to cease use of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic good.

children.aqlPath: /content[openEHR-EHR-INSTRUCTION.medication_order.v0]/activities[at0001]/description[at0002]/items[at0113]/items[at0013 and name/value='Date of end of treatment']/value

localizedDescriptions.en: Directions about the dispensing of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic good.
An additional instruction directed primarily at the person dispensing the medication, vaccine or therapeutic good.
Details related to the amount of a medication, vaccine or other therapeutic item to be supplied or supplied to the patient, as part of authorisation, dispensing or administration.
The amount of medication, vaccine or therapeutic good intended to be supplied or actually supplied.
The dose unit or pack unit associated with the dispense amount.
Permission for substitution with a bioequivalent medication.
Substitution of the Medication with a bioequivalent medication is allowed.
Substitution of the Medication with a bioequivalent medication is not allowed.

annotations.comment: In many jurisdictions, substitution of a prescribed medication, vaccine or other therapeutic good as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases subsitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.

localizedDescriptions.en: Additional narrative about the medication order not captured in other fields.
Unique identifier for the medication order.
